chemistry.scope_summary: Good to excellent yields for meta- and para-substituted reactants. Ortho-substituted reactants gave slightly diminished yields. Alkylthiols showed similar reactivity to arylthiols. N-heterocyclic reactants resulted in somewhat lower yields. Compatible with aryl halides.

optimization_table.key_findings: [Ru(bpy)₃Cl₂]·6H₂O with tBuONO gives best yield of 86%

figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Comparison of various methods for constructing aromatic C – S bonds.
Scheme 2.  Substrate scope of the photocatalytic Stadler–Ziegler process.
Scheme 2.  Substrate scope of the photocatalytic Stadler–Ziegler process.

figures.flow_path: Two syringe inputs merge into capillary microreactor under LED irradiation
figures.key_information: Safer handling of diazonium intermediates, homogeneous irradiation of reaction medium, short reaction times
figures.caption: Figure 1.  a) Schematic representation of the microfluidic setup used for the continuous-flow synthesis of arylsulfides through a photocatalytic Stadler–Ziegler reaction. b) Picture of the capillary microreactor irradiated with blue LED light.
Scheme 3.  Formation of biarylsulfides through a photocatalytic Stadler–Ziegler reaction in continuous-flow.

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Light activation of Ru(bpy)3^2+ to excited state *Ru(bpy)3^2+
Three different pathways (A, B, C) for C-S bond formation
Dimerization reaction to form disulfide product 4
N2 elimination in pathways A and B
figures.caption: Scheme 4.  Possible mechanisms for the photocatalytic Stadler–Ziegler reaction.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction times (60 min), lower yields (59%), safety concerns with explosive diazosulfide intermediates
translation_logic.flow_advantage: 78-fold improvement in productivity, shorter residence time (15 min), higher yield (79%), safer handling of intermediates through continuous processing
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 60 to 15 minutes, light source changed from fluorescent to LED, addition of nitrogen atmosphere, reactor changed to capillary microreactor
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Temperature (25°C), solvent (MeCN), catalyst system and loadings
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Safer handling of explosive diazosulfide intermediates through continuous processing and minimized accumulation

figure_descriptions: Schematic representation of microfluidic setup with syringe pumps feeding capillary microreactor under blue LED irradiation
Substrate scope tables showing yields for various aryl thiols and anilines
Mechanistic schemes showing photoredox catalytic pathways

conflicts: Pass3 reported pump.number_of_inlets as 1, but reactor scheme figure shows 2 syringe inputs - used figure value (2)
notes: Good quality extraction with clear batch-to-flow translation. Reactor scheme provides high confidence structural information. Some missing quantitative details like flow rates and concentrations reduce overall confidence.
